sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Girona FC has struggled in their last 5 matches, with no wins and only 1 draw, conceding heavily (11 goals). Levante UD also shows poor form with no wins in their last 4 matches, though they’ve managed to score in most games (5 goals). Both teams are in the relegation zone, indicating a lack of consistency.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head matches, Levante UD has a slight edge with 1 win and 1 draw against Girona FC. Both matches saw goals from both sides, suggesting a likelihood of a competitive, goal-scoring encounter.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Girona FC and Levante UD rank poorly in goals scored (both with 2) and conceded (11 each), showing defensive vulnerabilities. Girona’s low conversion rate (16.67%) and Levante’s slightly better attacking output in recent games hint at a marginal edge for Levante in creating chances.

sections.subtitle: Key Metrics
sections.text: Both teams struggle with clean sheets, and head-to-head trends show both teams often score. Yellow card stats (referee average 5.1 per game, head-to-head trend of over 4.5 cards) suggest a physical game. Corners are typically under 10.5 in their matchups, aligning with recent individual stats.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Levante UD leads Girona FC by a score of 0-1 at Estadi Montilivi. The sole goal came from Etta Eyong in the 43rd minute, capitalizing on a well-executed attacking play. Levante has dominated much of the game, controlling possession and creating more threatening opportunities. Girona, on the other hand, has struggled to find their rhythm, and their situation is compounded by a red card to Axel Witsel, leaving them with 10 players for the remainder of the match.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Levante UD has shown superior control in the first half, holding the majority of ball possession and making frequent entries into the final third. Their attacking intent was clear with multiple big chances created, though they missed one significant opportunity. Defensively, they have been solid, winning all their aerial duels and maintaining a balanced approach in ground duels. Girona FC, in contrast, has been on the back foot. Despite some entries into the penalty area, they failed to register a single shot on target and created no big chances. Their defensive frailties were exposed late in the half, and the red card further hampers their ability to reorganize effectively.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Disciplinary challenges
halftimeSections.text: Discipline has been a major issue for Girona in this first half. With three yellow cards and a red card, their aggression has cost them dearly, especially with Witsel’s dismissal. This numerical disadvantage will likely impact their ability to mount a comeback. Levante, while also picking up a couple of yellow cards, has managed to avoid more severe sanctions, allowing them to maintain their full squad for the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw distinct shifts in attacking momentum between the two sides. Girona started with some early intent, pushing forward in the opening minutes with a few promising spells. However, Levante quickly took control, establishing a sustained period of dominance with consistent pressure in the attacking third. Their momentum peaked towards the end of the half, culminating in the goal just before the break. Girona struggled to regain any significant attacking rhythm after the initial minutes, with their efforts stifled by Levante’s control and their own reduced numbers.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Levante, Etta Eyong has been the standout performer, not only scoring the crucial goal but also posing a constant threat upfront. Jeremy Toljan’s contribution with an assist highlights his importance in creating opportunities from the back. On Girona’s side, Yáser Asprilla showed some attacking intent and could be a focal point if they are to find a way back into this match. Joel Roca also displayed moments of quality in midfield, which might be crucial for any second-half resurgence despite the numerical disadvantage.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Levante UD appears well-positioned to maintain their lead and potentially extend it. Their dominance in possession and ability to create chances, combined with Girona’s reduced squad, gives them a clear edge. Girona will need to adopt a more defensive posture while seeking counter-attacking opportunities, but their lack of shots on target in the first half raises doubts about their ability to turn things around. A tactical adjustment will be critical for them to avoid further punishment. Levante, meanwhile, should look to exploit the extra space and capitalize on their numerical advantage.
